baseline: DoD ATO readiness starter mapping for NIST SP 800-53 Rev. 5 / FedRAMP High / DoD Cloud SRG
system: TokenDNA AI Agent Identity Control Plane
boundary: Customer-local control plane, collector, SDK integrations, data stores, audit export, and deployment automation

controls.title: Account Management
controls.implementation_summary: Tenant API keys carry explicit roles; admin key lifecycle routes require tenant RBAC and emit audit events. Enterprise SSO and SCIM group-role mappings support customer-owned account sources.

controls.title: Access Enforcement
controls.implementation_summary: FastAPI dependencies enforce role thresholds across protected routes using least-privilege TenantContext defaults.

controls.title: Least Privilege
controls.implementation_summary: API keys default to readonly, legacy keys are migrated to readonly, and only owner actors can mint owner keys.

controls.title: Event Logging
controls.implementation_summary: Security events are written to a hash-chained audit log and can be forwarded to SIEM webhooks.

controls.title: Protection of Audit Information
controls.implementation_summary: Audit entries are HMAC protected and hash chained. WORM/object-lock retention is a deployment responsibility documented in backup and ATO runbooks.

controls.title: Continuous Monitoring
controls.implementation_summary: CI, preflight, dependency audit, operator SLO, and evidence collection scripts produce repeatable monitoring artifacts for assessor review.

controls.title: Baseline Configuration
controls.implementation_summary: Helm values, production env templates, release manifests, and preflight profiles define the deployable baseline. Customer-owned infrastructure baselines must be inherited or attached in eMASS.

controls.title: Configuration Settings
controls.implementation_summary: DoD compliance profiles enforce FIPS, managed secrets, SIEM audit forwarding, mTLS material, TLS data service settings, and KMS/HSM signing posture.

controls.title: Identification and Authentication
controls.implementation_summary: OIDC/JWT verification supports explicit tenant claims and role/group mapping. CAC/PIV integration is inherited through the customer IdP or gateway.

controls.title: Authenticator Management
controls.implementation_summary: API keys are generated with high entropy, stored only as hashes, and revoked through audited admin flows. Secret storage and rotation are enforced by profile gates and customer KMS/Vault operations.

controls.title: Incident Handling
controls.implementation_summary: Incident playbooks cover breach response, key rotation, mass revocation, forensic audit verification, and customer communications.

controls.title: Vulnerability Monitoring and Scanning
controls.implementation_summary: CI runs lint, full tests, pip-audit, CodeQL, TruffleHog, Trivy, Helm validation, and production preflight.

controls.title: Developer Configuration Management
controls.implementation_summary: Release manifests, CI gates, dependency pins, and signed-artifact placeholders support controlled software release evidence.

controls.title: Transmission Confidentiality and Integrity
controls.implementation_summary: mTLS is enforced for internal service-to-service traffic: data stores (Redis/ClickHouse/Postgres) via MTLSConfig, and the internal API plane (:8443) via a TLS 1.3-only, client-cert-REQUIRED listener (mtls_server) with SPIFFE peer authorization (mtls_peer.require_internal_peer). cert-manager issues and auto-rotates the internal PKI (internal-pki.yaml).

controls.title: Device Identification and Authentication
controls.implementation_summary: Internal-plane peers authenticate with X.509 client certificates pinned to the internal CA; the peer's SPIFFE URI SAN is authorized against an allowlist (mtls_peer.require_internal_peer), so a valid cert is necessary but not sufficient. Mutual TLS is CERT_REQUIRED at the :8443 listener.

controls.title: Cryptographic Key Establishment and Management
controls.implementation_summary: Trust authority supports software, AWS KMS, CloudHSM, and HSM backends; DoD profiles require managed KMS/HSM style backends.

controls.title: Cryptographic Protection
controls.implementation_summary: Federal build flavor (Dockerfile.fips, UBI9) runs the CMVP-validated Red Hat OpenSSL FIPS provider; modules.security.fips.assert_fips_mode() fail-closes (exit 78) at startup when REQUIRE_FIPS=true but the validated provider is not active. A CI grep gate forbids non-approved hash primitives in modules/, and the release fips-smoke job proves the commercial image fails the gate while the federal flavor is fail-closed. The commercial distroless flavor remains non-FIPS by design.
controls.cmvp_certificate: Red Hat Enterprise Linux 9 OpenSSL Cryptographic Module — CMVP #4746 (UBI9). PIN: confirm the certificate number against the exact openssl build in the released registry.access.redhat.com/ubi9 image digest at release time; the validated cert number is version-specific.

controls.title: Protection of Information at Rest
controls.implementation_summary: Database, object storage, backup, and volume encryption are inherited from the customer-managed IL4/IL5/IL6 enclave or approved cloud service offering.

controls.title: Flaw Remediation
controls.implementation_summary: Pinned dependencies, blocking vulnerability audit, CodeQL, Trivy, and release gates support repeatable flaw remediation evidence.

controls.title: Provenance
controls.implementation_summary: Release manifests record git SHA, artifacts, runbooks, and required gates. SBOM and signed provenance artifacts are expected release inputs.
